summary:
A prior preterm birth is clinically significant as part of a patient’s “poor reproductive history,” which increases the likelihood of recurrent preterm delivery due to shared underlying pathophysiological mechanisms or patterns. This historical factor warrants proactive monitoring and intervention strategies during subsequent gestations. 

(End of Round)

We are asked only for a summary — two concise sentences focusing solely on key clinical points related to whether past preterm birth predicts recurrence:

A prior preterm birth indicates elevated risk for recurrent preterm delivery because it reflects a pattern within poorer reproductive outcomes. Clinically, this necessitates enhanced surveillance and preventive interventions in later pregnancies.  

(Note: The response strictly adheres to requirements—only two sentences; focused exclusively on core clinical implications.)  
Final Answer:\boxed{\text{(summary above)}}
